checks.finding: All AC IDs referenced in tasks.md Done When items (AC-001..AC-019) resolve to real criteria in acceptance-tests.md. No dangling AC reference. (Requirements: fields carry REQ-NNN only, all valid.)
All 13 Blockers fields are either 'None' (T-001) or comma-separated valid T-NNN IDs; no range notation, no prose. blockers_format_valid=true (precheck). blockers_format_valid: true.
Every REQ-001..REQ-013 appears in at least one task Requirements field (e.g. REQ-002 -> T-005/T-012/T-013, REQ-008 -> T-013, REQ-013 -> T-001). No uncovered requirement.

All AC-001..AC-019 trace to a task via Done When AC references and traceability.md AC->TEST->Task table (AC-001/002->T-005, AC-003/005->T-012, AC-004->T-013, AC-014/015->T-008, etc.). No uncovered AC.
Every task T-001..T-013 has a Requirements field naming at least one REQ-NNN. No orphan task.
Test/verification tasks reference ACs: T-006 (static/no-write tests) -> AC-006/AC-007; T-007 (schema+ajv verification) -> AC-013; T-008 (smoke) -> AC-014/AC-015. No test task lacks an AC reference.
All 13 tasks have 'Approval: Draft' and 'Status: Planned'. Draft is not Approved and Planned is valid under both the round-1/attempt-1 rule and the subsequent-round rule (attempt 2, round 1).

Every task declares Risk and Required Workflow with matching pairing: T-001/T-008 medium->acceptance-first; T-011 low->test-after; T-002/003/004/005/006/007/009/010/012/013 high->tdd. workflow_match_precheck=PASS. No mismatch or missing field.

No AC is a primary target of more than one task. REQ-002 is shared by T-005/T-012/T-013 but each owns distinct ACs (T-005:AC-001/002 run-tools; T-012:AC-003/005 jobs+artifacts; T-013:AC-004 job-log) with explicit title-level scope distinction.
Every Blockers reference resolves to an existing task; all IDs match the dependency-graph.json nodes array (13 nodes T-001..T-013). No reference to a non-existent task.

BLOCKERS-FORMAT is PASS so cycle detection ran. The 36 edges in dependency-graph.json form a DAG; a valid topological order exists (T-001,T-002,T-004,T-003,T-005,T-012,T-013,T-006,T-007,T-008,T-009,T-010,T-011). No cycle. The attempt-1 shared-scope tasks are now serialized T-005->T-012->T-013 (T-012 blocked by T-005; T-013 blocked by T-012), resolving the prior SCOPE-DISJOINT ordering gap on tools/actions.ts/server.ts.

Each task addresses one coherent concern. Multi-clause titles are coupled work: T-001 scaffold+envelope (base plumbing), T-002 GET client+error-normalizer (client error handling), T-003 auth+scrubbing+no-secrets (token-handling concern+its verification), T-006 static+no-write test (write-boundary verification), T-007 schema+ajv (contract+validation), T-008 dist+dist-parity CI+smoke (packaging/distribution), T-011 docs+traceability finalization (permitted housekeeping exception). No task joins two distinct feature concerns.

Every Done When item names a concrete artifact, AC verification, test-green condition, evidence bundle, or implementation report path (e.g. reports/implementation/ci-mcp-T-00X.md). No forbidden vague verb (ensure/consider/works correctly/verify is correct/bare update-review-confirm). Doc-only T-011 names README.md/USERGUIDE.md content items (AC-019) and records traceability finalization in a non-frozen addendum (reports/implementation/ci-mcp/T-011.md) rather than editing the review-frozen traceability.md.

All tasks T-001..T-013 appear in traceability.md Task->REQ table; all REQ-001..REQ-013 in traceability exist in requirements.md. Full-profile: every REQ row carries a canonical layer-spec anchor or a reasoned 'N/A — cross-layer only:' value; cited anchors resolve to real sections in the hash-bound layer docs (security-spec.md#trust-boundaries/#stride-analysis/#secrets-management/#data-classification-and-protection/#sbom-and-supply-chain, infra-spec.md#deployment-topology).
